Tests if dividing NaN by positive infinity results in NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of dividing `Number.NaN` by `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` in JavaScript. It checks if the result of this division is `NaN` by using the `isNaN` function and asserts that the outcome is `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on verifying a specific mathematical operation involving special numeric values in JavaScript. It is likely part of a test suite to ensure the correct handling of `NaN` values in arithmetic operations.
